repos.name: almighty-blocker-unstoppable
repos.description: Almighty Blocker: The unstoppable hosts file enforcer. Built in Go, it locks down your redirect rules using native OS services (systemd/SCM) and a robust primary/watchdog heartbeat system to prevent bypasses.

repos.name: vitorhugo-java
repos.description: Config files for my GitHub profile.

repos.name: angular-animeseason-jikan
repos.description: A fast, responsive seasonal anime tracker built with Angular and powered by the Jikan API (MyAnimeList). Features an optimized, clean UI for browsing current and upcoming anime releases.

repos.name: navigable-outline-index-widget
repos.description: A Flutter widget that provides a synchronized, navigable outline index for scrollable lists, allowing easy section jumping and current position highlighting.

repos.name: java-faces-quick-start
repos.description: A production-ready Jakarta EE 10 kickstarter for building Java Faces applications. Ships with a complete login system out-of-the-box — registration, e-mail verification, password reset — so you can focus on building your features instead of your auth plumbing.

repos.name: BroadcastSenderFX
repos.description: Um BroadcastSender to upload one or more file to several HTTP POST APIs.

repos.name: herojorney
repos.description: Desafio: desenvolver uma aplicação web que abrace a temática de heróis e, ao mesmo tempo,  seja a plataforma perfeita para você demonstrar todo o seu talento em desenvolvimento front- end e criatividade.

pinnedRepos.name: SpringAPI_EspacoGeek
pinnedRepos.description: A robust Spring Boot backend powering Espaço Geek. Features a comprehensive GraphQL API for managing geek culture media libraries (Anime, Movies, TV Series, Games), user watch-lists, JWT authentication, and automated data processing pipelines with Spring Batch.

pinnedRepos.name: NextJS_EspacoGeek
pinnedRepos.description: The modern, responsive frontend application for the Espaço Geek platform, built with Next.js and React. It consumes a robust GraphQL API via Apollo Client and features a dynamic UI styled with Tailwind CSS, built-in internationalization (i18n), JWT-based authentication, and user profile management for tracking geek culture media (Anime, Movies etc)

pinnedRepos.name: SpringBoot-JobApplyTracker
pinnedRepos.description: ⚙️ Robust REST API for the JobApplyTracker system. Built with Spring Boot and MariaDB, featuring JWT authentication, Flyway migrations, and comprehensive monitoring with Prometheus & Grafana.

pinnedRepos.name: React-JobApplyTracker
pinnedRepos.description: 🚀 A modern, responsive frontend application to track and manage job applications. Built with React, Vite, and Tailwind CSS, it features a comprehensive dashboard, application status tracking, and authentication.

issue.title: A lightway link shortener minimalist writting in Go, build with Postgress for Scalability
issue.body:
IA NOTE: Act as a Senior Backend Engineer. Build a high-performance URL shortener in Go (Fiber v3) using PostgreSQL and Redis.

1. ARCHITECTURE & URL LOGIC:
   - Primary Logic: The route should be `GET /:slug/:target_url+`. 
   - Note: Use Fiber's wildcard or parameter capturing to ensure that everything after the slug (including protocols, domains, and complex query strings like ?u=1&z=2) is captured as the destination.
   - Behavior: If a user visits `e.hugojava.dev/my-link/google.com?q=golang`, the app must treat 'my-link' as the slug and 'https://google.com?q=golang' as the target.
   - Response: Return a minimalist Dark Mode HTML page with a "Copy Link" button for `e.hugojava.dev/my-link`.

2. DATABASE (PostgreSQL 18 + JSONB):
   - Table Schema: 
     * id (UUIDv7 or Serial)
     * slug (VARCHAR, Unique Index)
     * original_url (TEXT)
     * metadata (JSONB) - Store custom UI settings or tags.
     * analytics (JSONB) - Store an array of click objects (timestamp, IP, User-Agent, Referrer).
   - Use 'pgx/v5' as the PostgreSQL driver for maximum performance and native JSONB support.

3. CACHING (Redis):
   - Every new link must be cached in Redis with the slug as the key.
   - Redirection Route (`GET /:slug`):
     * Check Redis. If it's a hit, asynchronously update the PostgreSQL analytics JSONB and redirect.
     * If it's a miss, query PostgreSQL, populate Redis, and then redirect.

4. DOCKER & ENVIRONMENT:
   - Provide a multi-stage Dockerfile (Go build -> Alpine/Scratch runtime).
   - Provide a docker-compose.yml including the Go App, PostgreSQL 18, and Redis.
   - Ensure PostgreSQL uses a persistent volume for the data directory.

5. CODING STANDARDS:
   - Strictly DO NOT include any comments inside the source code.
   - Use environment variables for all sensitive configurations (DB_URL, REDIS_URL).
   - Implement a clean "Copy to Clipboard" feature using Vanilla JS in the response HTML.
   - Implement a basic Rate Limiter middleware.
